summary_one_line: A well-formed scheme://host:port URL (e.g. pop3://...:900) that survives parsing and connect so curl_multi_remove_handle is reached with an empty msglist (loop-exit false side).

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A byte string parseable as a valid scheme://host:port URL — the winner seeds carry a literal 'pop3' scheme token followed by a host and a ':900' port and a digit run, structured enough to drive the easy handle through the multi_runsingle state machine down to curl_multi_remove_handle, where the message-queue loop at line 891 exits immediately (false side) because no pending message targets this handle.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds contain the literal scheme bytes 'pop3' at offset 0x06 and a ':900' / port-like digit run (':::::::900' / ':9001') in the 0x10-0x1f region, plus an embedded 'HTTP/' request line — the structured URL parses and connects, so execution reaches line 891 and takes the false (loop-exit) side; the per-role overlay marks line 891 [W] resolving and the cmplog winner shows hit-count 2 on the branch.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are short, repetitive byte runs ('pk+++++*++', '%`%a>f', long '%%%%' / 'AAAA' fills) with no coherent scheme://host:port structure; they fail to parse/connect, so they never reach line 891 with an empty msglist and instead stall earlier in the state machine (loser blocked the branch in 8/10 trials).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog / value_profile_cmplog add I2SRandReplace, which reads intercepted CMP operands (scheme-string compares, port-integer compares along the URL parse and connect path) from the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the expected operand into the input, synthesizing the structured 'pop3://...:900' URL that naive/value_profile cannot stumble onto by havoc; this lets only the I2S-equipped arm drive execution deep enough to reach and exit the message-queue loop.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is a for-loop over the multi message list whose globally blocked side is false.
Both decisive pairs differ only by the I2S technique.
The second decisive pair is cmplog over naive, also an I2S delta.
Winner-resolving seeds contain a literal pop3 scheme token and port-like digit runs.
A winner seed embeds an HTTP request line, evidence of structured URL/protocol content.
Line 891 is reached and exits the loop on the winner side (W-only) but the loser takes the true side.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 891  T=0 F=4  T=20 F=0  for(e = multi->msglist.head; e; e = e->next) {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Loser-blocking seeds are short repetitive byte runs lacking URL structure.
cmplog and vpc add the I2SRandReplace mutator that substitutes one intercepted CMP operand into the input.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile (the loser of pair 1) has no I2SRandReplace mutator.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 308 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 2 --seed faf6b58d07527f1a` and observed 1 I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 22 of the 23-level ancestor chain, the lower-bound signal of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find in cmplog under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating only on a structured scheme://host:port URL parse, where cmplog/vpc show no advantage over naive/value_profile in reaching the loop-exit (false) side — i.e. naive resolves it at the same rate as cmplog.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact CMP operand that I2S solves (scheme string vs. port integer vs. something deeper in connect) is inferred from seed bytes and the deep call chain, not pinned to a specific intercepted comparison.
